scope.basis: current recorded test results and existing latest-run/profile/contract artifacts

firstRunPolicy.rule: for a site's first observed run, execute all known tools, compare outputs, normalize task fields, choose the best surviving result set, and record preferred + fallback order

keyFindings: browser-type route currently wins in recorded evidence
scrapy-cffi and curl-cffi can sometimes obtain page material but quality/field extraction is weaker or less stable
playwright and playwright-stealth hit Amazon error pages in the recorded sample
current evidence is dominated by robot/human verification pages
do not treat Walmart as stable anonymous production extraction yet
needs another strict same-basis all-tools rerun before promoting any single tool path
local-agent-browser-cli is the only clearly usable route in the latest recorded matrix
http-oriented paths tend to be shell-heavy, partial, or blocked
Temu should currently be treated as browser-first rather than generic crawler-first
anonymous results consistently fall into login, slider, punish, nocaptcha, or x5secdata flows
1688 anonymous profile must not be treated as a stable production extraction target
authenticated mode, if authorized, must be a separate browser-based profile and run history

authBoundary.credentialHandling: use credentials only inside one normal browser login flow
notAllowed: credential spray across all crawler stacks

remainingBlockers: a fresh strict same-basis all-tools rerun is still needed to put all four sites on one synchronized matrix baseline
field normalization is still heuristic and should be refined toward per-task or per-site schema extraction

notes: no real credentials received or stored
1688 auth is modeled only as an authorized browser workflow with manual intervention when challenged
